name: INFOCOM Ltd (ТОВ "Інфоком Лтд")
description: INFOCOM Ltd is a Ukrainian SME founded in 1996, specializing in industrial automation (АСУ ТП), SCADA systems, building automation, IoT solutions, and AI/machine vision. The company is a certified Siemens Solution Partner — the first in Ukraine (since 2014). With ~90-125 employees, offices in Zaporizhzhia, Kyiv, Uzhhorod (Ukraine), Dolný Kubín (Slovakia), and Wrocław (Poland), INFOCOM has completed 1,000+ projects across 40+ countries. Annual revenue ~$14M. Core competencies span industrial process control (Siemens PCS7, TIA Portal, WinCC), energy management, EV charging infrastructure (UGV Chargers brand), energy storage systems, building automation, robotics (TIMA robot, GNOM UGV), and neural network-based machine vision. The company qualifies as an SME under EU definition (<250 employees, independent enterprise).

coreTeam.name: Eduard Trotsenko
coreTeam.experience: 30 years in industrial automation. Founded INFOCOM in 1996. Built the company from 5 employees to 90-125+ staff across 5 offices in 3 countries. Also CEO of Temerland (defense robotics).

coreTeam.name: Vitaliy Gorbyk
coreTeam.experience: Technical leadership of all engineering projects. Oversees automation systems department and technology stack.

coreTeam.name: Sergey Lebedenko
coreTeam.experience: Leads the core engineering team responsible for automation system design, PLC/SCADA programming, commissioning, and integration.

coreTeam.name: Sergey Opara
coreTeam.experience: Leads IT/software division. Responsible for MES, data analytics, web applications, and mobile apps.

coreTeam.name: Alexander Ivanov
coreTeam.experience: Leads engineering design and documentation for automation and electrical projects.

coreTeam.name: Sergey Rumyantsev
coreTeam.role: Head of Unmanned Technology Department / UGV Chargers R&D Lead
coreTeam.experience: Leads both unmanned technologies (TIMA robot, defense UGVs) and UGV Chargers R&D. Expert in autonomous systems, sensor fusion, and power electronics.

coreTeam.name: Sergey Zakharchenko
coreTeam.experience: Manages in-house production facilities for automation cabinets, switchgear, and hardware.

previousProjects.title: Oil Extraction Plant Automation (Cargill, Siemens Oil & Gas, etc.)
previousProjects.description: PCS7 and WinCC development for oil extraction and refinery plants across Netherlands, Belgium, France, Germany, UK (Liverpool), Spain, Turkey, Zambia, and other countries. Including PCS7 v9.1 migrations.

previousProjects.title: Central Treatment Facilities Dispatching (Ashgabat, Turkmenistan)
previousProjects.description: Dispatching system for 300 water treatment plants, centralized monitoring and control.

previousProjects.title: Grain Terminal Automation (Ukraine)

previousProjects.title: UGV Chargers — EV Charging Network

previousProjects.relevance: IoT platform development, cloud-connected devices, real-time monitoring, energy management, OCPP protocol expertise

previousProjects.title: UGV Storage — Energy Storage Systems
previousProjects.description: Distributed autonomous energy storage clusters (100-1200 kW) based on lithium-ion batteries in containerized modules with inverters, cooling, and fire suppression.

previousProjects.title: Building Automation (Poland office)
previousProjects.description: Building automation systems, parking control, autonomous boiler houses (rooftop/independent heating), RFID identification, water treatment automation, autonomous power supply (solar + conventional hybrid).

previousProjects.title: Metallurgical Plant Energy Balance (Kryvyi Rih)

certifications: Siemens Solution Partner Automation (2014 — first in Ukraine)

partnerships.description: Strategic technology partnership since 1996. INFOCOM is a certified Siemens Solution Partner, Technology Partner, and SIVACON licensed manufacturer. Core platform for industrial automation projects.
partnerships.relevance: Provides access to Siemens industrial IoT ecosystem, PLCs, SCADA, and building automation technologies

partnerships.description: Partner for SCADAPack and ClearSCADA solutions, complementing Siemens stack.
Industrial sensor and measurement instrumentation partner.
partnerships.relevance: Sensor integration expertise for IoT deployments

strengths: 25+ years of sensor-to-actuator integration experience (core of SmartLoop's closed-loop automation)
Siemens Solution Partner — deep PLC/SCADA/BMS expertise directly applicable to building automation
Production PROFIBUS/PROFINET/OPC UA experience — industrial protocol stack overlaps with IoT protocols needed for COP-PILOT
Building automation already offered via Poland subsidiary (boiler houses, parking, RFID, energy systems)
Energy management proven track record (metallurgical plant energy balance, solar, storage systems)
AI/ML deployed in production (99% accuracy wagon recognition, TIMA robot autonomous navigation)
IoT platform development experience (UGV Chargers: cloud-connected devices, OCPP, mobile app, billing)
EU legal entities in Slovakia and Poland — simplifies EU project administration
Remote monitoring of distributed systems (300 treatment plants in Turkmenistan, gas wells)
Hardware + software integration capability (not just software — can deploy physical systems)
Located in Zaporizhzhia — same city as ZNU, enables tight consortium coordination
gaps: No FIWARE / NGSI-LD experience (but OPC UA and REST API experience provides foundation)
No prior EU-funded grant projects (commercial-only track record)
No Valencia/Spain presence or prior Spanish projects
Technology stack is Siemens-centric — adaptation to FIWARE open-source ecosystem needed
copilotRelevance.roleInConsortium: Technical lead for IoT integration, sensor-actuator deployment, building automation, energy monitoring, and hardware-software integration. INFOCOM's 25+ years of industrial automation with Siemens directly addresses the key gap identified in COP-PILOT reviews: lack of production IoT/SCADA experience.
